D9 BiologyL4 AnalyzeReference

Hummingbird Gorget — Melanin Platelet Stacks

a male Anna's hummingbird flashes electric pink at one angle and black at another — flat melanin platelets in barbules form a multilayer that's sharp in colour and sharp in direction

Hummingbird gorget barbules contain stacks of flat melanin platelets (~80 nm thick, ~80 nm spacing). Unlike Morpho's broad-angle design, these are specular — they reflect a narrow band only at a narrow angle. Rotate the bird and the gorget vanishes; line it up and it explodes with colour.

Drag viewing angle. Within ±10° the colour saturates; outside, it goes near-black.
